# Project Coding Conventions

## Naming

### File Naming
- **Pattern**: `PascalCase.swift` for all source files
- **Model files**: `{FeatureName}Model.swift` (e.g., `AwakeModel.swift`, `PortManagerModel.swift`)
- **View files**: `{FeatureName}View.swift` (e.g., `AwakeView.swift`, `PortManagerView.swift`)
- **Overlay files**: `{FeatureName}OverlayView.swift` (e.g., `ZoomItOverlayView.swift`, `ScreenRulerOverlayView.swift`)
- **Bridge/utility files**: Descriptive name + purpose (e.g., `FFmpegBridge.swift`)
- **Evidence**: All 47 Swift files follow this convention consistently

### Class/Struct Naming
- **Model classes**: `{FeatureName}Model` — `final class` with `ObservableObject` conformance (16/19 models are `final`)
  - Exception: `TestDataGeneratorModel` is `class` (not `final`), `MarkdownPreviewModel` inherits `NSObject`
- **View structs**: `{FeatureName}View` — `struct` with `View` conformance
- **Supporting types**: Named enums/structs placed at top of model file before the main class
  - Evidence: `ClipboardItemType`, `PortCategory`, `PortSortOrder`, `PortInfo`, `WebhookTopic`, `Phase`, `AnnotationTool`
- **NSView subclasses**: Not `final`, named descriptively (`CrosshairsOverlayView`, `FindMyMouseOverlayWindow`)
- **Enum naming**: `PascalCase` type, `camelCase` cases — most conform to `String, CaseIterable`
  - Evidence: 15 enums across features, 12 conform to `CaseIterable`

### Variable/Property Naming
- **camelCase** for all properties and variables
- **Boolean properties**: `is`-prefixed (`isEnabled`, `isRunning`, `isIndefinite`, `isHovered`)
- **Model references**: `model` (always) via `@EnvironmentObject var model: {FeatureName}Model`
- **Timer properties**: `{purpose}Timer` (e.g., `countdownTimer`, `pollTimer`, `refreshTimer`, `reconnectTimers`)

## File Structure

### Directory Organization
```
MacPowerToys/
├── MacPowertoysApp.swift      # App entry point
├── ContentView.swift          # Main hub + navigation
├── Assets.xcassets/           # Asset catalog
├── MacPowertoys.entitlements  # Entitlements
└── Features/
    └── {FeatureName}/         # One directory per feature
        ├── {FeatureName}Model.swift
        ├── {FeatureName}View.swift
        └── (optional overlay/detail/bridge files)
```

- **Feature isolation**: Each feature is self-contained in its own directory
- **No shared utilities directory**: Helper types are defined inline in model files
- **Evidence**: 16 feature directories, each following the same structure

### Feature Module Sizes
| Feature | Files | Notes |
|---------|-------|-------|
| Standard (12 features) | 2 | Model + View only |
| ScreenRuler, ZoomIt | 3 | + OverlayView (NSView) |
| ScreenAnnotation | 4 | + OverlayView + ToolbarView |
| PortManager | 3 | + PortDetailView |
| VideoConverter | 3 | + FFmpegBridge |
| MouseUtilities | 9 | 4 sub-features (FindMyMouse, Highlighter, Crosshairs, CursorWrap) |

## Import Order

- **No enforced order** — mixed approaches observed
- **Dominant pattern**: System frameworks first, no blank line separation
- Most common imports by frequency:
  1. `AppKit` (26 files)
  2. `SwiftUI` (24 files)
  3. `Foundation` (19 files)
  4. `UniformTypeIdentifiers` (6 files)
  5. `Carbon.HIToolbox` (4 files)
- **View files** typically import only `SwiftUI`
- **Model files** import `Foundation` + `AppKit` + domain-specific frameworks
- **Evidence**: grep across all 47 source files

## Error Handling

- **Dominant pattern**: Graceful degradation — errors are logged but not propagated to UI
- **`do/catch` with logging**: Used for JSON encoding/decoding, file I/O
  - Log format: `NSLog("[ModuleName] Failed to <action>: %@", error)` or `print("[ModuleName] <message>")`
- **`try?` for non-critical operations**: File operations, optional data conversions
- **No `try!` usage observed** in feature code
- **Process execution errors**: stderr redirected to `FileHandle.nullDevice`, errors caught silently
- **Evidence**: `ClipboardManagerModel.swift`, `WebhookNotifierModel.swift`, `PortManagerModel.swift`, `FFmpegBridge.swift`

## Async Patterns

- **Timer-based operations**: `Timer.scheduledTimer` with `[weak self]` capture + `Task { @MainActor in }` inside callback
- **Process bridging**: `withCheckedContinuation` to bridge `Foundation.Process` to async/await
- **Background work**: `DispatchQueue.global(qos: .userInitiated)` for heavy operations
- **All UI updates**: Must go through `@MainActor` — all model classes are annotated `@MainActor`
- **No Combine usage** — pure SwiftUI `@Published` + manual `Timer`/`DispatchQueue`
- **Evidence**: All 19 model classes annotated `@MainActor`; Timer closures use `Task { @MainActor in ... }`

## Logging

- **Mixed approach** (inconsistency):
  - `NSLog` in 5 files (ClipboardManager, MarkdownPreview, FFmpegBridge, VideoConverter, WebhookNotifier)
  - `print` in 5 files (ColorPicker, PortManager, ScreenAnnotation, ScreenRuler, ZoomIt)
  - 37 files have no logging
- **Log prefix format**: `[ModuleName]` prefix in all log messages (consistent)
  - Evidence: `[ClipboardManager]`, `[ColorPicker]`, `[ScreenAnnotation]`, `[PortDetail]`
- **No structured logging framework** (e.g., `os.log`, `Logger`)

## Comments & Documentation

- **No doc comments** (/// or /** */) — zero instances found across the entire codebase
- **MARK comments**: Used extensively in model files for section organization
  - 27/47 files use `// MARK: -` sections
  - Common sections: Settings, Runtime State, Private, Monitoring, Init, Lifecycle
  - **View files rarely use MARK** — only PomodoroTimerView and a few complex views
  - Evidence: AwakeModel (6 MARKs), PomodoroTimerModel (12 MARKs), ScreenAnnotationModel (21 MARKs)
- **Inline comments**: Rare, used only for complex parsing logic (PortManagerModel nettop parsing)
- **Style**: Self-documenting through clear method and property names

## Access Control

- **Dominant pattern** across all model classes:
  ```
  @Published var settingProp       // public: user-facing settings
  @Published private(set) var      // read-only: derived/runtime state
  private var internalState        // private: timers, buffers, internal state
  ```
- **Methods**:
  - `func startMonitoring()` / `func stopMonitoring()` — always public (internal access)
  - `private func` — all helper/internal methods
- **No explicit `public` or `internal` keywords** — relies on Swift's default `internal` access
- **View structs**: All properties use default access except `@State private var` for local state
- **Evidence**: Consistent across all 19 model files

## Persistence

- **UserDefaults** for all settings persistence (8/19 model files use it)
- **Key naming**: `"{featureName}.{propertyName}"` dot-notation
  - Evidence: `"clipboardManager.isEnabled"`, `"pomodoroTimer.focusMinutes"`, `"markdownPreview.isDarkTheme"`
- **Pattern**: `didSet { UserDefaults.standard.set(newValue, forKey: "...") }` on `@Published` properties
- **Loading**: In `init()` with `UserDefaults.standard.bool/integer/object(forKey:)`
- **Complex data**: `JSONEncoder`/`JSONDecoder` with `Codable` types stored as Data in UserDefaults
- **No `@AppStorage`** usage — all UserDefaults access is manual
- **No Core Data or SwiftData**

## UI Design System

### Navigation Header (Universal)
```swift
HStack {
    Button { onBack() } label: {
        Label("Back", systemImage: "chevron.left")
            .labelStyle(.iconOnly)
    }
    .buttonStyle(.plain)
    Text("Title")
        .font(.system(.headline, design: .rounded))
    Spacer()
}
Divider()
```
- Evidence: All 16+ feature views use this identical pattern

### Design Tokens
| Token | Value | Evidence |
|-------|-------|---------|
| Font design | `.rounded` | All views |
| Button style | `.buttonStyle(.plain)` | All views |
| Toggle style | `.toggleStyle(.switch)` | All toggle-based features |
| Primary spacing | `12` | VStack in all views |
| Secondary spacing | `6-10` | Inner groups |
| Corner radius (cards) | `14-16` | CompactFeatureCard, FeatureCardView |
| Corner radius (elements) | `6-7` | Buttons, badges, icons |
| Background opacity | `.white.opacity(0.14)` | Icon backgrounds |
| Stroke opacity | `.white.opacity(0.18)` | Icon borders |
| Hover state | `.quaternary.opacity(0.45 → 0.85)` | Cards |
| Control size | `.controlSize(.mini)` | Toggles |

### View Callback Pattern
- All feature views accept `let onBack: () -> Void` closure for navigation
- Evidence: Every `{FeatureName}View` struct declares this property

### No Preview Providers
- Zero `#Preview` or `PreviewProvider` found across all view files
